title: Arlong’s Rule Tightens Around Nami
scene: On pages 1–4, the story moves through Arlong Park and Nami’s dangerous position among the fish-men. Cocoyasi Village and nearby towns show the cost of Arlong’s occupation.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The bounty on my head is the highest in all of East Blue at 20 million belli.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Considering that,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: It's only natural that a well-known bounty hunter would come for me.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: So where is this RORONOA Zoro hiding...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: He's not the most classy guy,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: If he thinks he can sneak in here and cut down my brethren while I'm out...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I... I don't know! I really have nothing to do with this Zoro guy you're talking about! Please let me go!

dialogue.speaker: Chew
dialogue.text: CHU!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: It's undeniable that you personally attacked Arlong as well.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: There's not a chance that we'd let you free.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Alright, I admit it! M-me and Zoro are real tight bros! S-S-so you better not lay a single finger on me!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Or else Zoro will come and kill all of you!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Talk about a change in attitude.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Oh? so you mean to say that Zoro will come if I kill you?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Who!? N-No! That's not it! Killing me would be the wrong thing to do!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: If you keep me alive, Zoro should definitely come to save me!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: In fact, if you don't keep me alive, he'll never come!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Hey Nami! Say something!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: What's wrong, Nami? You look a little troubled, no?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: It seems like you're still doubting me quite a bit...!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's because I know what you're like that I'm doubting you.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: You're a little too cunning.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Damn it!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I'm not going to let myself...be killed because of some stupid misunderstanding.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Why does everything need to go wrong now of all times!!...?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: just/when I'm so close to achieving my goal...!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Nami! I'm real disappointed in you!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: you witch!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Luffyy didn't doubt you for a single second,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Even after he heard you ran away!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: He comp- lately trusts you! Even now!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: But to think you've been deceiving him all Arlong without even batting an eye!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Sorry, but the only thing I trust is money.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Blame his idiocy for being duped.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: What did you just say!?

summary: This part builds the Arlong Park conflict by showing how Nami’s choices look contradictory from outside while Arlong’s power keeps the island trapped.
